sections.heading: Architecture Overview
sections.content:
The indexer is the core engine of cortex. It scans a project directory, builds a knowledge graph of nodes (functions, modules, documents, configs) and edges (composes, delegates_to, validates, documents), and stores everything in `.cortex/cortex.db` (SQLite).

```
cortex/
  cli.py                # Click CLI â€” thin wrappers over index/*
  models.py             # Pure dataclasses: CortexNode, CortexEdge, CortexIndex, StepMarker
  ontology.py           # Loads cortex_ontology.yaml, validates edge types
  index/
    builder.py          # Orchestrates scanning â†’ upsert pipeline
    db.py               # SQLite schema, upsert_node, query helpers, FTS5
    runner.py           # Pytest runner detection, report format selection
    junit_parser.py     # Parses JUnit XML and pytest-json-report output
    test_mapper.py      # Maps pytest IDs â†’ cortex node IDs via validates edges
  scanners/
    module_scanner.py   # AST-based Python scanner: functions, classes, modules
    doc_scanner.py      # Markdown doc scanner
    json_doc_scanner.py # DocJSON scanner: sections, links â†’ edges
    config_scanner.py   # Config scanner: .claude/ settings, skills, hooks â†’ config nodes
```

The pipeline is: `cortex init` (full baseline) or `cortex build` (discovery-only) â†’ scanners produce `CortexNode`/`CortexEdge` lists â†’ `builder.py` orchestrates upserts â†’ `db.py` writes to SQLite with incremental change detection.

All read paths (CLI commands, viz server, MCP tools) go through `db.py` query functions. No business logic lives outside the index package.

sections.heading: Sequence of Operations
sections.content:
### cortex build (discovery-only, default)
```
cli.cmd_build(project_root)
  â†’ builder.build(root, discovery_only=True)
    â†’ module_scanner scans all .py files via AST
      â†’ Per file: parse â†’ walk AST â†’ extract functions/classes
      â†’ Compute code_hash (SHA-256[:16] of body) and desc_hash (of docstring)
      â†’ Generate CortexNode per function + composite_process per module
      â†’ Generate composes edges (module â†’ function)
      â†’ Detect dFlow decorators â†’ store in dflow_meta
      â†’ Test functions (test_ prefix / test file) get subtype='test'
    â†’ json_doc_scanner scans docs/**/*.json
      â†’ Doc files â†’ composite_process/subtype=docjson
      â†’ Doc sections â†’ atomic_process/subtype=docjson
         code_hash = prose body hash, desc_hash = heading hash
      â†’ Parse sections, extract links â†’ documents edges
    â†’ config_scanner scans .claude/ and other config dirs
      â†’ Settings, skills, hooks â†’ composite_process/subtype=config
      â†’ Whole-file hashing for change detection, mtime fast-pass
      â†’ No section parsing â€” each file is a single node
    â†’ For each node: db.upsert_node()
      â†’ Compare code_hash + desc_hash against stored values
      â†’ If unchanged: skip (returns False)
      â†’ If changed: write + append node_history row (CODE_ONLY/DESC_ONLY/CODE_AND_DESC)
      â†’ If new: write + append INITIAL history row
    â†’ For each edge: db.upsert_edge()
    â†’ compute_staleness(db_path, project_root)
      â†’ Shared staleness engine writes staleness column for all nodes
    â†’ Return summary dict {nodes_written, nodes_skipped, edges_written, ...}
```

### cortex check
```
compute_staleness(db_path, project_root):
  â†’ Shared staleness engine in cortex/index/staleness.py
  â†’ Re-reads files, computes hashes, writes staleness column
  â†’ Returns summary: N CONTENT_STALE Â· N DESC_STALE Â· N LINKED_STALE Â· N STRUCTURAL_DRIFT Â· N CLEAN
  â†’ See staleness design spec for full algorithm
```

sections.heading: Data Model & Graph Schema
sections.content:
### Core Tables

| Table | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `nodes` | All indexed entities: functions, modules, docs, external packages. Includes `staleness TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'CLEAN'` column (ADR-009). |
| `edges` | Typed relationships: composes, delegates_to, validates, documents, etc. |
| `tags` | Many-to-many nodeâ†”tag |
| `node_fts` | FTS5 virtual table over level_1/level_2 for full-text search |
| `node_history` | Append-only change log per node (INITIAL, CODE_ONLY, DESC_ONLY, etc.) |
| `node_verification` | Verification records: who verified, at what `code_hash_at` and `desc_hash_at` (ADR-009 added `desc_hash_at`). |
| `node_renames` | Tracks old_id â†’ new_id when functions are renamed |
| `docs` | DocJSON file-level records |
| `doc_sections` | Individual sections within DocJSON files |
| `test_runs` | Pytest run metadata (timestamp, git SHA, duration, summary) |
| `test_results` | Per-test results linked to test_runs and optionally to cortex nodes |

### Node Types (from ontology â€” ADR-009 unified)

Three structural `node_type` values with required `subtype`:

| node_type | subtype | What it represents |
|---|---|---|
| `composite_process` | `module` | Python module |
| `composite_process` | `docjson` | DocJSON doc file |
| `composite_process` | `external_package` | External dependency |
| `atomic_process` | `function` | Python function or method |
| `atomic_process` | `docjson` | DocJSON doc section |
| `atomic_process` | `test` | Test function |
| `entity` | â€” | Data artifact |

`document` and `constraint` are retired as node_type values.

### Edge Types (from ontology â€” ADR-009 updated)

| Edge Type | From â†’ To | Meaning |
|---|---|---|
| `consumes` | process â†’ entity | Input data flow |
| `produces` | process â†’ entity | Output data flow |
| `composes` | composite â†’ process | Structural containment |
| `delegates_to` | composite â†’ process | Runtime invocation |
| `depends_on` | process â†’ process/entity | Execution dependency |
| `constrains` | atomic_process â†’ process/entity | Schema/contract restriction (was `constraint â†’`) |
| `validates` | atomic_process â†’ process | Test coverage (was `process/constraint â†’`) |
| `documents` | atomic_process/composite_process â†’ any | Documentation link (was `document â†’`) |
| `supersedes` | atomic_process/composite_process â†’ same | Replacement/obsolescence (was `process/constraint â†’`) |

sections.heading: Implementation Trade-offs
sections.content:
### AST-based scanning over regex
Python's `ast` module correctly handles nested functions, decorators, multi-line docstrings, and async defs. Regex would fail on all of these. The cost is Python-only support for per-function granularity; non-Python files get whole-file hashing.

### Incremental upserts with change detection
`upsert_node` compares `code_hash + desc_hash` before writing. If both match, the row is skipped entirely â€” no write, no history row. This keeps `cortex build` fast on unchanged projects (< 1s on ~300 nodes) and prevents history pollution.

### Discovery-only as the default build mode
The default `cortex build` only inserts new nodes and never updates existing ones. This preserves DESC_STALE signals that haven't been resolved yet. To reset all baselines, use `cortex init` â€” there is no `--full` flag on `cortex build`.

### cortex init as the safe first-run and reset command
`cortex init` creates the database, runs a full scan, and establishes baselines for every node. If the database already exists, it prompts for confirmation before wiping and rebuilding. This separates the destructive reset path from the safe everyday `cortex build` path.

### FTS5 with three-stage fallback
Search uses: (1) FTS5 ranked search, (2) LIKE-AND substring fallback, (3) LIKE-OR broad fallback. This ensures something always comes back, even for malformed queries.

sections.heading: Technical Verification
sections.content:
### Existing Tests
Unit and integration tests exist in the cortex test suite covering:
- Module scanner: function extraction, hash computation, dFlow decorator detection
- DB layer: upsert_node change detection, history row generation, FTS search
- Builder: end-to-end build on fixture projects
- Staleness: mtime fast path, hash-based detection, verification promotion
- Test runner: JUnit parsing, test-mapper edge resolution

### Edge Cases to Cover
- Nested functions (inner function hashes must not leak into outer)
- Renamed functions (STRUCTURAL_DRIFT detection + node_renames tracking)
- Empty docstrings vs missing docstrings (different desc_hash values)
- Unicode in function names and docstrings
- Files with syntax errors (should degrade to STRUCTURAL_DRIFT, not crash)
- Concurrent builds (SQLite single-writer â€” should fail gracefully)
